skill.name: vue-testing-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Vue.js testing with Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Pinia, async components, Suspense, Teleport, and Playwright E2E workflows.
skill.summary: Apply practical Vue testing patterns for reliable unit, component, async, store, and E2E tests.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic from network, command, script, and environment patterns. Manual review found documentation examples, markdown formatting, local test configuration, and official reference links, with no evidence of malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Benign shell commands in testing documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged shell command patterns, but the reviewed lines are package installation and test runner examples for Vue, Vitest, and Playwright workflows. These commands are relevant to the skill purpose and do not include download-and-execute chains, credential access, or suspicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside markdown code fences and matches normal developer setup instructions. No command uses user-controlled interpolation, secret reads, or external exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dynamic imports are Vue async component examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings are examples for defineAsyncComponent and async component testing. They import local component files in documentation and are not runtime code executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use local Vue component imports in fenced JavaScript snippets. The surrounding text teaches test timing and module handling, not dynamic remote code loading.

low_findings.title: Environment access is CI configuration only
low_findings.description: The process.env findings are Playwright and Vitest configuration examples that branch on CI mode. They do not read secrets, environment files, tokens, or credentials, despite the analyzer labeling some lines as sensitive access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment variable observed is CI, used to tune retries, workers, server reuse, and threading. No evidence found for credential access or environment file reading.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are references and localhost examples
low_findings.description: The URL findings are official documentation links, a copyright attribution URL, localhost test server examples, and illustrative API paths. They do not transmit data or instruct the assistant to contact an untrusted endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documentation, attribution, localhost, or placeholder application paths. No code path sends secrets or collected data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography detections lack supporting evidence
low_findings.description: The cited weak cryptography locations are markdown frontmatter, descriptions, or ordinary testing snippets. Manual review found no hashing, encryption, random token generation, or cryptographic API use at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not contain cryptographic code or algorithm names. The finding appears to be caused by broad keyword matching in markdown content.

low_findings.title: Critical heuristic combines unrelated benign examples
low_findings.description: The critical dangerous-combination heuristic is not confirmed. The network, command, and environment patterns occur in separate educational examples and do not form a credential access, network exfiltration, or code execution workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are normal Playwright setup, CI configuration, localhost configuration, and test runner commands. No semantic link connects them into malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Vue test suites often fail because async rendering, stores, Teleport, and browser behavior are handled inconsistently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for reliable Vue tests.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Vitest setup patterns for Vue 3 projects using Vite.
Explains blackbox component testing with user-facing assertions.
Guides async test handling with nextTick, flushPromises, Suspense, and dynamic components.
Shows Pinia store and composable testing setup patterns.
Compares node-based runners, browser-mode tests, and Playwright E2E tests.
limitations: Does not execute tests or inspect live browser output by itself.
Does not replace project-specific test architecture decisions.
Examples use simplified component names and mock data.
Package versions may need adjustment for each repository.

use_cases.title: Set up a Vue test stack
use_cases.description: Choose Vitest, Vue Test Utils, jsdom or happy-dom, and package scripts for a new Vue 3 project.

use_cases.title: Fix flaky async tests
use_cases.description: Identify missing awaits, unresolved promises, async setup issues, and dynamic import timing problems.

use_cases.title: Review component test quality
use_cases.description: Replace brittle implementation tests and snapshot-only tests with behavior-focused assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a test runner
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Vue 3 testing setup for this project. Compare Vitest, Vue Test Utils, browser mode, and Playwright for my needs.

prompt_templates.title: Fix an async component test
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this failing Vue async component test. Explain where I need nextTick, flushPromises, Suspense, or a dynamic import mock.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Pinia and composable tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these Vue tests for Pinia stores and composables. Use createTestingPinia, setActivePinia, and helper wrappers where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Audit the whole test strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Vue repository test strategy. Find brittle implementation tests, snapshot-only tests, async race risks, and gaps needing Playwright.

output_examples.input: A component test fails before async setup finishes.
output_examples.output: The response explains that the component needs a Suspense wrapper, waits for pending promises, and asserts on rendered behavior.

output_examples.input: A Pinia store test throws an injection error.
output_examples.output: The response recommends the correct Pinia test setup and explains when to stub actions or run real actions.

output_examples.input: A suite relies only on snapshots.
output_examples.output: The response identifies weak coverage and proposes behavior-focused assertions for visible text, events, links, and accessibility queries.

best_practices: Prefer behavior-focused assertions over component internals.
Await Vue updates, user interactions, and external promises explicitly.
Use browser or E2E tests only when real browser behavior matters.
anti_patterns: Using snapshot-only tests as proof that a component works.
Testing private component state instead of visible behavior.
Expecting node DOM simulators to validate real CSS or native browser behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill write Vue tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and guidance that an AI assistant can apply when editing tests in your repository.

faq.question: Which tools does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Testing Library patterns, Pinia testing, and Playwright E2E testing.

faq.question: Can it help with flaky async tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers nextTick, flushPromises, Suspense wrappers, async setup, and dynamic component loading.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains documentation links and example localhost URLs, but it does not make network requests.

faq.question: Can it compare browser and node test runners?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when node simulators are enough and when browser-mode or Playwright tests are better.

faq.question: Is this useful for existing test suites?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps review brittle tests, missing awaits, weak snapshots, and incorrect Pinia or composable setup.
